title: File-Delivery Outcomes and Typed Evidence · Contract Lens: Transfer Evidence Plan
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing typed file-delivery outcome dossier; use discriminated result unions and exhaustive changed-outcome evaluation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transfer evidence plan increment in typed file-delivery outcome dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan deterministic browser models, both strict TypeScript compiler paths, runtime schema tests, Node stream and handle checks, controlled AWS integration, load, fault, restore, security, cost, and production evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set observable availability, latency, integrity, freshness, recovery, accessibility, privacy, and cost targets with measurement windows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Transfer Evidence Plan · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3412 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan deterministic browser models, both strict TypeScript compiler paths, runtime schema tests, Node stream and handle checks, controlled AWS integration, load, fault, restore, security, cost, and production evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transfer Evidence Plan contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Transfer Evidence Plan evidence · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-transfer-evidence-plan-xbmarw" that uses case 7454 to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost. Concept boundary: contrast one accepted path, one near miss, and the claim that still needs transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
